matched_testcase_text: test case id: tc-authentication-001 title: user logs in successfully module: authentication source pages: 2 preconditions: user has registered an account. test data: - email: test@example.com - password: testpassword test steps: 1. user initiates login request to the application. 2. application forwards the request to the user management system. 3. user management system checks if the user is logged in. 4. user management system requests firebase authentication api to get the current user. 5. firebase authentication api returns the current user. 6. user management system confirms the user is logged in and forwards the user object to the application. 7. application displays the user's profile. expected result: user is successfully logged in and redirected to their profile.

item_text: flow 2: sistema gestione utente -> sistema di gestione dei dati -> user=checkiflogin()
matched_testcase_text: test case id: tc-authentication-002 title: user logs in unsuccessfully due to incorrect password module: authentication source pages: 2 preconditions: user has registered an account. test data: - email: test@example.com - password: wrongpassword test steps: 1. user initiates login request to the application. 2. application forwards the request to the user management system. 3. user management system checks if the user is logged in. 4. user management system requests firebase authentication api to get the current user. 5. firebase authentication api returns null as the user does not exist. 6. user management system confirms the user is not logged in and prompts the user to try again. 7. application displays an error message. expected result: user is prompted to try logging in again due to incorrect password.

matched_testcase_text: test case id: tc-authentication-003 title: user registers and logs in successfully module: authentication source pages: 2 preconditions: user has not registered an account. test data: - email: newuser@example.com - password: newpassword test steps: 1. user initiates registration and login request to the application. 2. application forwards the request to the user management system. 3. user management system requests firebase authentication api to create a new user. 4. firebase authentication api creates a new user. 5. user management system confirms the user is logged in and forwards the user object to the application. 6. application displays the user's profile. expected result: user is successfully registered and logged in.

item_text: it includes the flow of messages between the application, user management system, data management system, and firebase authentication api.
matched_testcase_text: test case id: tc-authentication-001 title: user logs in successfully module: authentication source pages: 2 preconditions: user has registered an account. test data: - email: test@example.com - password: testpassword test steps: 1. user initiates login request to the application. 2. application forwards the request to the user management system. 3. user management system checks if the user is logged in. 4. user management system requests firebase authentication api to get the current user. 5. firebase authentication api returns the current user. 6. user management system confirms the user is logged in and forwards the user object to the application. 7. application displays the user's profile. expected result: user is successfully logged in and redirected to their profile.

item_text: it includes the flow of messages between the application, user management system, data management system, and firebase authentication api.
matched_testcase_text: test case id: tc-authentication-003 title: user registers and logs in successfully module: authentication source pages: 2 preconditions: user has not registered an account. test data: - email: newuser@example.com - password: newpassword test steps: 1. user initiates registration and login request to the application. 2. application forwards the request to the user management system. 3. user management system requests firebase authentication api to create a new user. 4. firebase authentication api creates a new user. 5. user management system confirms the user is logged in and forwards the user object to the application. 6. application displays the user's profile. expected result: user is successfully registered and logged in.

matched_testcase_text: test case id: tc-authentication-001 title: user logs in successfully module: authentication source pages: 2 preconditions: user has registered an account. test data: - email: test@example.com - password: testpassword test steps: 1. user initiates login request to the application. 2. application forwards the request to the user management system. 3. user management system checks if the user is logged in. 4. user management system requests firebase authentication api to get the current user. 5. firebase authentication api returns the current user. 6. user management system confirms the user is logged in and forwards the user object to the application. 7. application displays the user's profile. expected result: user is successfully logged in and redirected to their profile.
